docs.text: Information Architecture Information Architecture (IA) is the structure behind effective documentation. Key concepts: Content modeling Metadata Taxonomy Navigation Chunking for LLMs Findability & discoverability
docs.title: Basics

docs.text: DocOps: CI/CD Pipeline This site is deployed using: GitHub Actions MkDocs GitHub Pages Automated deployment to gh-pages branch Documentation-as-Code practices Whenever content is updated and pushed to the main branch: GitHub Actions creates a fresh Ubuntu VM Installs MkDocs Builds the site Pushes the generated HTML to the gh-pages branch GitHub Pages updates the live site Note: For GitHub pages to reflect the changes real time, Build and Deployment > Source will have to be set as 'Deploy From Branch' followed by gh-pages > '/root', this will have to be done first time only to activate pages afterwards set it back to GitHub Actions.
docs.title: Pipeline

docs.text: This site is deployed using: GitHub Actions MkDocs GitHub Pages Automated deployment to gh-pages branch Documentation-as-Code practices Whenever content is updated and pushed to the main branch: GitHub Actions creates a fresh Ubuntu VM Installs MkDocs Builds the site Pushes the generated HTML to the gh-pages branch GitHub Pages updates the live site Note: For GitHub pages to reflect the changes real time, Build and Deployment > Source will have to be set as 'Deploy From Branch' followed by gh-pages > '/root', this will have to be done first time only to activate pages afterwards set it back to GitHub Actions.
